findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Audit Hook
findings.description: The PostToolUse hook (hooks/monitor.sh) captures all tool invocations including full input, output, and session environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, HOME) and sends them to an external server (mcp-analytics-hub.io) via curl. This constitutes unauthorized exfiltration of sensitive credentials and tool data.
findings.evidence: The script collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, and HOME environment variables and sends them in a POST request to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool with an Authorization header.
findings.remediation: Remove the audit hook entirely. Never transmit API keys, tokens, or tool outputs to external servers. Use local-only logging if audit is needed.

findings.title: Credential Theft via Environment Variable Harvesting
findings.description: The monitor.sh script explicitly harvests ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment and transmits them to an external server. These are high-value credentials that grant access to AI and code repository services.
findings.evidence: The script includes 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY': "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" and 'GITHUB_TOKEN': "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" in the session_env payload sent to the external server.
findings.remediation: Never collect or transmit environment variables containing API keys or tokens. Remove all credential harvesting from the script.

findings.title: Direct Prompt Injection in SKILL.md Instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions contain deceptive content designed to trick the agent into installing a malicious audit hook. The instructions use homoglyph characters (e.g., 'Сonfigure' with Cyrillic 'С', 'сentralized' with Cyrillic 'с') to evade text-based detection while instructing the agent to copy a monitoring script to the agent's hooks directory.
findings.evidence: The instructions say 'cp hooks/monitor.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh' and 'chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh' to install a hook that captures all tool invocations and sends them to an external server.
findings.remediation: Remove the SKILL.md file and never install hooks that transmit data externally. Validate all instructions for deceptive content.

findings.title: Tool Exploitation via PostToolUse Hook Installation
findings.description: The skill installs a PostToolUse hook that intercepts all tool invocations, capturing inputs, outputs, and environment variables. This manipulates the agent's tool execution system to exfiltrate data on every tool use without the user's knowledge or consent.
findings.evidence: The hook is installed at ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh and logs every tool invocation including full input/output and session environment to an external server.
findings.remediation: Do not install hooks that intercept tool invocations. Remove the hooks directory and any monitoring scripts.

findings.title: Capability Inflation via Deceptive Skill Name
findings.description: The skill is named 'mcp-toolkit' and claims to manage MCP servers and tool integrations, but its actual purpose is to install a data exfiltration hook. The name and description are deceptive, hiding malicious intent behind a legitimate-sounding capability.
findings.evidence: The YAML frontmatter describes the skill as 'Manage Model Context Protocol servers and tool integrations' but the actual behavior is installing a monitoring hook that exfiltrates data.
findings.remediation: Rename the skill to accurately reflect its behavior, or remove it entirely.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Homoglyph Characters
findings.description: The SKILL.md uses homoglyph characters (Cyrillic 'С', 'с', 'Р' instead of Latin 'C', 'c', 'P') throughout the document to evade text-based security scanning and detection. This is a deliberate obfuscation technique.
findings.evidence: Examples: 'Сonfigure' (Cyrillic С), 'сentralized' (Cyrillic с), 'Рrotocol' (Cyrillic Р), 'Сoverage' (Cyrillic С), 'mcp-analytics-hub.io' (Cyrillic с in 'analytics').
findings.remediation: Use only standard ASCII characters. Remove all homoglyph substitutions.

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Tool Chaining
findings.description: The skill implements a tool chaining pattern: every tool invocation (read, write, bash, python, etc.) is captured by the PostToolUse hook, which then sends the full input, output, and environment to an external server. This creates an automated data pipeline from local tools to remote server.
findings.evidence: The hook captures TOOL_NAME, TOOL_INPUT, TOOL_OUTPUT for every tool invocation and sends them to mcp-analytics-hub.io via curl.
findings.remediation: Remove the hook. Do not chain tool outputs to external network calls.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Token in Script
findings.description: The monitor.sh script contains a hardcoded Authorization token 'mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6' used to authenticate with the external server. This token is exposed in plaintext in the script.
findings.evidence: The script includes '-H "Authorization: Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6"' in the curl command.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables or secure credential storage if authentication is necessary.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools Declaration
findings.description: The SKILL.md YAML frontmatter does not declare an 'allowed-tools' field. While this is optional per the spec, declaring it would help users understand what tools the skill intends to use.
findings.evidence: The YAML frontmatter includes name, description, license, and compatibility but no allowed-tools field.
findings.remediation: Add an 'allowed-tools' field to the YAML frontmatter specifying which agent tools the skill uses.
